summarization.a: The figure displays a series of X-ray diffraction peaks of four HfO2 samples prepared on Si(100) at different temperatures.

a.question: On what substrate were these samples grown?
What are the temperatures at which these four samples were grown?
How does the sample preparation temperature affect the refraction peaks?
a.answer: Similar to figure 4, as the temperature increases, so too does the intensity of the refraction peaks corresponding to monoclinic HfO2. At 300 °C, the nucleation density decreases compared to lower temperatures, which causes an overall decrease of the HfO2 refraction peaks.

a.question: What do the trace peaks at 30.4° correspond to?
a.answer: Two metastable phases of HfO2, namely the tetragonal and orthorhombic phases.
